texto: You do not carry the cross. Instead you are all crucified on the timber of your sufficiency, which is given to you, the more you insist, the more you bleed: it suits you to say you carry the cross like a sacred duty, whereas you are heavy with the weight of your necessities. Have the courage not to admit those necessities and lift yourselves up for your own sakes.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Carlo Michelstaedter: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de enfrentar incertezas com ação. O trecho “You do not carry the cross. Instead you are all crucified on the timber of your sufficiency, whic…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que coragem, crucifixion, way-to-persuasion pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de crucifixion e way-to-persuasion.
